g.text: Customer Issue Resolution System (Annotated for Interview)
Trace: "Customer raises a billing issue. System auto-categorizes, assigns to agent via routing, agent resolves, customer rates resolution."
"Customer calls support or raises a ticket.
— single API for raising issues, routing
and resolution. Hides all internal wiring."
— tier affects SLA and routing priority.
agents by department for fast lookup."
STEP 3 - Issue is the central object.
STEP 5 - Who handles this issue? (KEY STEP)
Billing dept, TECHNICAL to Tech dept.
Insert in chain. Nothing else changes."
Pattern: Chain of Responsibility | OCP, SRP
"Each issue has an SLA deadline based
STEP 7 - Issue not resolved in time.
agent ratings. Low score can trigger
load balance -> agent assigned. Issue moves through states (**State
Pattern**): Open -> Assigned -> InProgress -> Resolved -> Closed.
Facade, Chain of Responsibility, State, Strategy (x3), Observer
Issue: category, priority, SLA deadline, comments, resolution
Open -> Assigned -> InProgress -> Resolved -> Closed (+ Escalated, Reopened)
Route: category -> priority -> tier -> least loaded agent
Customer, agent, SLA watchdog, dashboard, audit notified
Customer rates resolution, CSAT score computed
